mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising aggregate income, which directly supports consumer spending and corporate earnings. This fundamental strength systematically drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS does not increase month-over-month in more than 60% of instances over any 3-year rolling window, or if the Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 20-day forward returns is below 0.05, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at or below 0.5%, monetary policy is extremely accommodative. This reduces discount rates applied to future cash flows, compresses risk premiums, and encourages equity investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after FEDFUNDS drops to or below 0.5%, the subsequent 20-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 40% of triggered cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in nonfarm payrolls and retail sales indicates synchronized strength in both the labor market and consumer demand. This dual signal provides more robust evidence of economic expansion than either series alone, reinforcing positive earnings expectations and boosting equity prices over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the combined signal (average of z-scores of PAYEMS and RSAFS) does not achieve a Rank IC of at least 0.06 with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession regime characterized by unemployment above 8% and accommodative policy, rising housing starts signal a durable recovery in residential investment. Residential investment leads the broader economy by several quarters, thus rising HOUST in this regime predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST increases by more than 5% month-over-month during the specified regime but S&P 500 60-day returns are not positive in at least 70% of instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production indicate expanding manufacturing activity and economic momentum. Over short horizons (5 trading days), equity markets respond positively to these real economic signals as they improve near-term earnings visibility.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases over a month but the average S&P 500 5-day forward return is not positive over any 5-year rolling period, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9%, it signals severe labor market distress, reduced disposable income, and heightened risk of a recessionary spiral. This condition systematically depresses equity valuations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If unemployment rises above 9% but the S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive in more than 25% of triggered cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of a decline in industrial production and an increase in the unemployment rate over a 3-month window indicates a coordinated contraction in supply and labor demand. This joint signal is a powerful harbinger of economic recession, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5% and UNRATE month-over-month change > 0.2%
failure_condition: If the joint trigger does not result in a mean 20-day forward return that is negative and statistically significant (t-test p<0.05) over a 10-year backtest, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession regime with extremely low interest rates (FEDFUNDS <= 0.5%) and high unemployment (UNRATE > 8%), the combined uptrend in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls provides a compelling signal that a self-sustaining recovery is underway. This interaction amplifies the positive outlook for corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 returns higher over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If during the specified regime, the combined uptrend (both HOUST and PAYEMS rising for at least 3 consecutive months) is followed by positive 60-day returns less than 60% of the time, reject.
